dna.description: Capgemini is a global leader in consulting, technology services, and digital transformation. Headquartered in Paris, the company partners with enterprises worldwide to transform and manage their business by harnessing the power of technology, from strategy and design to engineering and operations. Listed on Euronext Paris (CAP, CAC 40 component), Capgemini employs over 340,000 people across 50+ countries and reported revenues of EUR 22.5 billion in 2024.
dna.sub_industry: IT Consulting / Digital Transformation / Technology Services
dna.business_model: B2B Professional Services + Managed Services + SaaS Partnerships
products: Sogeti (technology and engineering services, local touch)
Cloud Infrastructure Services (hybrid and multi-cloud management)
Customer First (CX transformation and digital marketing)
Capgemini Insights & Data (data analytics and AI)
features: Global delivery model with nearshore/offshore centers
AI and generative AI at scale consulting
dna.pricing_model: Time-and-materials + fixed-price projects + managed service contracts (multi-year). Rate cards vary by geography and seniority. Enterprise framework agreements for strategic accounts.
dna.company_size_signals: 340,000+ employees, operations in 50+ countries, 2024 revenue of EUR 22.5 billion. One of the world's largest IT services companies. CAC 40 component on Euronext Paris.
content_themes: Future of work and talent transformation
dna.tone_of_voice: Professional, forward-looking, globally minded, with emphasis on partnerships, sustainability, and human-centric innovation

market.market_size_estimate: Global IT services market: ~USD 1.3 trillion (2024). IT consulting specifically: ~USD 350 billion. Digital transformation services: ~USD 880 billion. European IT services market: ~USD 400 billion.
market.growth_trend: Growing at 6-8% CAGR, accelerated by generative AI adoption, cloud migration mandates, cybersecurity investment, and enterprise modernization backlogs. European digital sovereignty initiatives creating additional demand.
likely_competitors: Accenture - largest global competitor with deep strategy and technology capabilities
TCS (Tata Consultancy Services) - Indian IT giant with strong enterprise delivery at competitive pricing
Infosys - major Indian IT services company with growing consulting arm
Wipro - IT services with strength in cloud and digital operations
Cognizant - US-headquartered IT services focused on healthcare and financial services
Atos - French IT services competitor (undergoing restructuring)
Deloitte Digital - consulting firm expanding into technology delivery
IBM Consulting - hybrid cloud and AI-focused consulting
competitive_advantages: Strong brand in France and continental Europe with deep government relationships
Capgemini Invent provides high-value strategy consulting
Capgemini Engineering (ex-Altran) adds unique R&D engineering capability
Balanced global delivery model (nearshore Europe + India + Latin America)
Top-tier partnerships with all major cloud and platform vendors
market_challenges: Intense pricing pressure from Indian IT services providers
Talent acquisition and retention in competitive markets
Commoditization of basic cloud migration and application management
Economic slowdown affecting discretionary IT spending
Need to continuously reskill workforce for emerging technologies
customer_pain_points: Difficulty finding a single partner for end-to-end transformation
Legacy IT estate blocking innovation and agility
Shortage of internal digital and cloud skills
Pressure to reduce IT costs while accelerating innovation
Slow time-to-value on large transformation programs
buying_triggers: Cloud migration deadline (end of data center lease, vendor EOL)
Major SAP ECC to S/4HANA migration need
Cost optimization initiative requiring IT outsourcing
Generative AI strategy development and deployment
decision_makers: VP of IT / Head of Infrastructure
market.sales_cycle_estimate: 2-4 months for staff augmentation and small projects, 6-12 months for managed services and transformation programs, 12-18 months for mega-deals (USD 100M+)
ideal_customer_characteristics: Enterprise with annual revenue above USD 1 billion
IT budget of at least USD 50 million per year
Operating in multiple countries requiring global delivery
Currently undergoing or planning digital transformation
Using SAP, Salesforce, or major cloud platforms (expansion opportunities)
Has complex legacy IT landscape needing modernization
Operates in regulated industry (financial services, healthcare, government)
Open to outsourcing and managed services model
Strategic focus on data, AI, and cloud adoption
Looking for a long-term strategic IT partner, not just a vendor
anti_icp_signals: Small or mid-size company with fewer than 500 employees (deal size too small)
Organization that only needs basic staff augmentation with no transformation agenda
Company with no IT budget or purely cost-cutting without transformation goals
Business with fully in-house IT philosophy and no outsourcing appetite
Startup with undefined processes and no enterprise IT infrastructure
Company locked into exclusive long-term contract with a competing IT services firm
Organization resistant to offshore or nearshore delivery models
expansion_opportunities: Generative AI consulting and implementation at scale
Industry-specific digital twins and intelligent operations
industry_trends: Generative AI transforming consulting delivery and client offerings
Increased focus on cybersecurity and digital resilience
European digital sovereignty and data regulations (DORA, NIS2, AI Act)
Intelligent automation reducing commodity service needs
Shift from project-based to outcome-based contracts
